schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 7eba745c005f7784b608e8ed903d11cd45a11119
findings:
  - { id: judged-cf-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 adopts a zone-agnostic reading of 'matching story' (a story resolving anywhere in the checkout's committed corpus, including specs/archive/, counts as a match) that contradicts parent dc-5's literal trigger condition: parent dc-5 fires the branch-presence disclosure for 'a stub card with no matching active story' and fixes disclosure text referencing 'this checkout's active store', a zone-restricted framing. Under the story's reading, a stub whose implementing story has been closed and archived renders as matched (no disclosure); under the parent's literal reading, that same stub would check refs/heads/design/\u003cslug\u003e and render the 'instantiated on design/\u003cslug\u003e, not yet in this checkout's active store' disclosure. These are observably different rendered behaviors for the same store state. No supersedes or exempts edge is declared against spec/workbench-legibility for this divergence — the story explicitly leaves it as prose disclosure only (citing ADJ-26's prohibition on declaring such an edge this round) and defers disposition to the wave-end cross-story review, so the conflict is real, acknowledged, and formally undeclared. (confidence 0.85)", target_ref: spec/workbench-legibility }
  - { id: judged-cf-2, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-3 compounds the cf-1 divergence: it claims its behavioral assertion 'proves the disclosure text dc-5 fixes' while substituting dc-1's zone-agnostic trigger for parent dc-5's own trigger ('no matching active story'). An e2e proof of the verbatim disclosure string under a trigger condition the parent did not specify verifies parent dc-5's TEXT but not its firing semantics — the story concedes this ('it does not, by itself, settle whether dc-1's zone-agnostic trigger is what parent dc-5 intended') yet declares no exempts edge against spec/workbench-legibility, leaving the parent's dc-5 trigger contract behaviorally unproven by this story's evidence plan with only prose acknowledgment. (confidence 0.55)", target_ref: spec/workbench-legibility }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/family-board-links#dc-1, spec/family-board-links#dc-2, spec/family-board-links#dc-3, spec/family-board-links#dc-4, spec/family-board-links#dc-5, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-1, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-2, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-3, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-4, spec/workbench-legibility#dc-5] }

Under the story's reading, a stub whose implementing story has been closed and archived renders as matched (no disclosure); under the parent's literal reading, that same stub would check refs/heads/design/\u003cslug\u003e and render the 'instantiated on design/\u003cslug\u003e, not yet in this checkout's active store' disclosure. These are observably different rendered behaviors for the same store state. No supersedes or exempts edge is declared against spec/workbench-legibility for this divergence — the story explicitly leaves it as prose disclosure only (citing ADJ-26's prohibition on declaring such an edge this round) and defers disposition to the wave-end cross-story review, so the conflict is real, acknowledged, and formally undeclared.\",\"confidence\":0.85,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-legibility\"},{\"id\":\"cf-2\",\"text\":\"Story dc-3 compounds the cf-1 divergence: it claims its behavioral assertion 'proves the disclosure text dc-5 fixes' while substituting dc-1's zone-agnostic trigger for parent dc-5's own trigger ('no matching active story'). An e2e proof of the verbatim disclosure string under a trigger condition the parent did not specify verifies parent dc-5's TEXT but not its firing semantics — the story concedes this ('it does not, by itself, settle whether dc-1's zone-agnostic trigger is what parent dc-5 intended') yet declares no exempts edge against spec/workbench-legibility, leaving the parent's dc-5 trigger contract behaviorally unproven by this story's evidence plan with only prose acknowledgment.\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-legibility\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-cf-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1 adopts a zone-agnostic reading of 'matching story' (a story resolving anywhere in the checkout's committed corpus, including specs/archive/, counts as a match) that contradicts parent dc-5's literal trigger condition: parent dc-5 fires the branch-presence disclosure for 'a stub card with no matching active story' and fixes disclosure text referencing 'this checkout's active store', a zone-restricted framing. Under the story's reading, a stub whose implementing story has been closed and archived renders as matched (no disclosure); under the parent's literal reading, that same stub would check refs/heads/design/<slug> and render the 'instantiated on design/<slug>, not yet in this checkout's active store' disclosure. These are observably different rendered behaviors for the same store state. No supersedes or exempts edge is declared against spec/workbench-legibility for this divergence — the story explicitly leaves it as prose disclosure only (citing ADJ-26's prohibition on declaring such an edge this round) and defers disposition to the wave-end cross-story review, so the conflict is real, acknowledged, and formally undeclared. (confidence 0.85)
- **judged-cf-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-3 compounds the cf-1 divergence: it claims its behavioral assertion 'proves the disclosure text dc-5 fixes' while substituting dc-1's zone-agnostic trigger for parent dc-5's own trigger ('no matching active story'). An e2e proof of the verbatim disclosure string under a trigger condition the parent did not specify verifies parent dc-5's TEXT but not its firing semantics — the story concedes this ('it does not, by itself, settle whether dc-1's zone-agnostic trigger is what parent dc-5 intended') yet declares no exempts edge against spec/workbench-legibility, leaving the parent's dc-5 trigger contract behaviorally unproven by this story's evidence plan with only prose acknowledgment. (confidence 0.55)
